sourceMovie.title: Thanksgiving
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Thanksgiving for the same reason it broke through in 2023: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a mystery rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Eli Roth keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The horror and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Scream
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Thanksgiving clicked for you, Scream (1996) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Thanksgiving?
faq.answer: Try Scream, Scream 2, Black Christmas—each shares DNA with Thanksgiving while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Thanksgiving?
faq.answer: Thanksgiving (2023) is best described as Horror, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Thanksgiving part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Thanksgiving Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
